BedrockAWSCredentials.description: AWS credentials for a Bedrock guardrail.

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID for static Bedrock guardrail credentials.

secret_access_key.description: Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged. The data plane passes it to the AWS SDK's static credentials provider.

BedrockLatencyMode.description: Per-guardrail latency policy for `kind: "bedrock"`. `serial` waits for the guardrail response. `timed` aborts at `timeout_ms` and applies `fail_open`.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the Bedrock guardrail response.

GuardrailHookPoint.description: What part of the request lifecycle a guardrail inspects.

oneOf.description: Run on the request payload before the upstream call.
Run on the upstream response before the cache write + render.
Run on both input and output.

KeywordPattern.description: Literal or regular-expression pattern used by a keyword guardrail.

action.description: Per-pattern action override: `mask` or `block`. Falls back to the guardrail's `default_action` when omitted.

name.description: Detector name surfaced in the mask token (`[<NAME>_REDACTED]`), telemetry counts, and block reasons. Never the matched value.

regex.description: Regular expression the DP compiles at chain build. Invalid patterns are logged and the row is skipped (same contract as keyword rules).

PiiDetectorConfig.description: One built-in detector selection for `kind: "pii"`. The `type` names a detector compiled into the DP (`aisix-guardrails::pii::BUILTIN_DETECTORS`); `action` optionally overrides the guardrail-level `default_action` for this detector only.

action.description: Per-detector action override: `mask` or `block`. Falls back to the guardrail's `default_action` when omitted.

PresidioEntityConfig.description: One entity selection for `kind: "presidio"`. The `type` names a Presidio entity (`EMAIL_ADDRESS`, `PHONE_NUMBER`, `PERSON`, `CREDIT_CARD`, …); `action` optionally overrides the guardrail-level `default_action` for this entity only — the same per-detector shape as `kind: "pii"`.

action.description: Per-entity action override: `mask` or `block`. Falls back to the guardrail's `default_action` when omitted.

description: Content policy evaluated before or after upstream calls.

patterns.description: Blocklist patterns. An empty list is valid and allows every request, equivalent to `enabled: false`.

oneOf.description: AWS Bedrock managed guardrail using `ApplyGuardrail` on input, output, or both.

guardrail_id.description: Guardrail identifier issued by the AWS console.

latency_mode.description: Bedrock guardrail latency policy. Use `timed` with `timeout_ms` to cap wait time.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled (the default), a Bedrock outage blocks model output instead of releasing unscanned content. The input hook continues to use the top-level `fail_open` policy.

region.description: AWS region for the Bedrock endpoint, such as `us-east-1`.

oneOf.description: Azure AI Content Safety Prompt Shield. Detects jailbreak and indirect injection attacks via the `/contentsafety/text:shieldPrompt` API.

api_key.description: Azure subscription key sent with the `Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` header. Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged.

endpoint.description: Azure Cognitive Services resource endpoint, e.g. `https://my-resource.cognitiveservices.azure.com`. The data plane appends `/contentsafety/text:shieldPrompt?api-version=2024-09-01`.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled (the default), an Azure outage blocks model output instead of releasing unscanned content. The input hook continues to use the top-level `fail_open` policy.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

oneOf.description: Azure AI Content Safety Text Moderation. Category-severity and blocklist moderation via the `/contentsafety/text:analyze` API, on input and/or output, including streaming output.

api_key.description: Azure subscription key sent with the `Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` header. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged.

blocklist_names.description: Azure CS blocklist names to match against.

endpoint.description: Azure Cognitive Services resource endpoint. The data plane appends `/contentsafety/text:analyze?api-version=2024-09-01`.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered in `buffer_full` mode before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to allow output when the buffer cap is hit.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled, an Azure outage does not release unscanned model output.

output_type.description: Severity scale. Use `FourSeverityLevels` for 0, 2, 4, and 6, or `EightSeverityLevels` for 0 through 7.

severity_threshold.description: General severity threshold. A category at or above it blocks.

severity_threshold_by_category.description: Per-category threshold overrides. These take precedence over the general threshold.

stream_processing_mode.description: `window` for sliding-window incremental release or `buffer_full` for whole-response hold-back.

text_source.description: Input-hook text selection. Use `concatenate_all_content` to include all message content. Ignored on the output hook.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

window_overlap_size.description: Chars carried between windows so a span split across a boundary is still caught.

window_size.description: Sliding-window size in characters for window mode.

oneOf.description: Aliyun content-safety guardrail. Risk-level moderation via the `TextModerationPlus` action on `green-cip.<region>.aliyuncs.com`, on input and/or output, including streaming output.

access_key_secret.description: Aliyun AccessKey secret. Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged. Used to sign the request.

endpoint.description: Explicit endpoint override as a full URL with no trailing slash. When set, it takes precedence over `region`.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered in `buffer_full` mode before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to allow output when the buffer cap is hit.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled, an Aliyun outage does not release unscanned model output.

region.description: Aliyun region the guardrail lives in, e.g. `cn-shanghai`. The data plane builds the endpoint `https://green-cip.<region>.aliyuncs.com`.

risk_level_threshold.description: Minimum risk level that triggers a block: `low`, `medium`, or `high`. A returned level at or above this blocks.

stream_processing_mode.description: `window` for sliding-window incremental release or `buffer_full` for whole-response hold-back.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

window_overlap_size.description: Chars carried between windows so a span split across a boundary is still caught.

window_size.description: Sliding-window size in characters when window mode is used. Aliyun limits each `llm_response_moderation` call to 2,000 characters.

oneOf.description: In-process sensitive-data detection + redaction (#932): built-in detectors + custom regex, per-detector `mask`/`block` actions, on input and/or output, including streaming output. Always available.

custom_patterns.description: Operator-supplied regex detectors, evaluated after the built-ins.

default_action.description: Action for detectors that don't set their own: `mask` (default) or `block`.

detectors.description: Built-in detectors to enable. Unknown detector ids are rejected at build time (row skipped + warn), so a typo can't silently disarm the policy.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered for a streamed response before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to release output unscanned (and unmasked) when the buffer cap is hit; the default `fail_closed` blocks the response instead.

oneOf.description: Lakera Guard screening via `POST /v2/guard` (#52): prompt-injection / jailbreak / content detection blocks; PII-only detections mask via the returned offsets, on input and/or output, including streaming output.

api_key.description: Lakera API key sent as a `Authorization: Bearer` header. Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged.

endpoint.description: Endpoint override, e.g. a regional or self-hosted Lakera deployment. The data plane appends `/v2/guard`. Defaults to `https://api.lakera.ai`.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered for a streamed response before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to release output unscanned when the buffer cap is hit; the default `fail_closed` blocks the response instead.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled (the default), a Lakera outage blocks model output instead of releasing unscanned content. The input hook continues to use the top-level `fail_open` policy.

project_id.description: Lakera project whose policy applies (`project-...`). Omitted → the account's default policy.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

oneOf.description: OpenAI Moderation API (#52): category content moderation via `POST /moderations`, detection-only (block, never rewrite), on input and/or output, including streaming output.

api_key.description: OpenAI API key sent as a `Authorization: Bearer` header. Decrypted before projection. Plaintext is held in memory only and is not logged.

category_thresholds.description: Per-category score thresholds, e.g. `{"violence": 0.5}`. When set, only the listed categories are enforced and a category blocks when its score reaches the threshold. When empty (the default), the API's own `flagged` boolean decides — the LiteLLM `openai_moderation` baseline behavior.

endpoint.description: Endpoint override (an Azure OpenAI deployment or a mock). The data plane appends `/moderations`. Defaults to `https://api.openai.com/v1`.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled (the default), an OpenAI outage blocks model output instead of releasing unscanned content. The input hook continues to use the top-level `fail_open` policy.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

oneOf.description: Self-hosted Microsoft Presidio PII detection + anonymization (#52): analyzer entities with per-entity `mask`/`block` actions and a selectable anonymize operator, on input and/or output, including streaming output.

analyzer_url.description: Presidio analyzer base URL, e.g. `http://presidio-analyzer:3000`. The data plane appends `/analyze`.

anonymizer_url.description: Presidio anonymizer base URL, e.g. `http://presidio-anonymizer:3000`. The data plane appends `/anonymize`. Only called when a detected entity's effective action is `mask`.

default_action.description: Action for entities that don't set their own: `mask` (default) or `block`.

entities.description: Entities to detect. Empty (the default) analyzes with Presidio's full recognizer set and applies `default_action` to every hit.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered for a streamed response before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: Buffer-overflow policy. Use `fail_open` to release output unscanned (and unmasked) when the buffer cap is hit; the default `fail_closed` blocks the response instead.

operator.description: Anonymize operator applied to masked entities: `replace` (default — Presidio substitutes `<ENTITY_TYPE>`), `mask` (asterisks), `hash` (SHA-256 hex), or `redact` (span removed).

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the output hook. When disabled (the default), a Presidio outage blocks model output instead of releasing unscanned content. The input hook continues to use the top-level `fail_open` policy.

score_threshold.description: Minimum analyzer confidence for a hit to count. Omitted → every result the analyzer returns counts (Presidio's own per-recognizer defaults apply).

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout in milliseconds, applied per analyzer/anonymizer call. `fail_open` and `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. A value of `0` triggers the timeout immediately.

created_at.description: RFC3339 creation timestamp. When present, guardrails are evaluated from oldest to newest. Resources without this timestamp sort after resources that have it.

direction.description: Attachment direction hint: `input`, `output`, or `both`. Stored for compatibility. Current hook selection still follows `hook_point`.

enabled.description: When false, the chain skips this rule entirely. Allows operators to stage a rule before enabling it.

enforcement_mode.description: How the data plane behaves when this guardrail fires. `monitor` is stored for compatibility but not yet enforced.

fail_open.description: Behavior when a remote API guardrail cannot reach its upstream. `true` allows the request and records the bypass reason in `usage_events.guardrail_bypassed_reason`. `false` blocks with 422. Keyword guardrails do not use this setting.

hook_point.description: Where in the lifecycle this rule runs.

mandatory.description: Whether guardrail evaluation errors are fatal. When `true`, a remote guardrail that can't reach its upstream blocks the request instead of failing open — it overrides `fail_open` on the failure path (the DP wraps the row in a MandatoryGuardrail that turns a `Bypass` into a `Block`). Default `false` keeps the `fail_open` behaviour.

name.description: Operator-facing name that surfaces in metric labels and error reasons.

title: Guardrail
